# FM cockpit event-kind chip system (pr / a2a / review / alert classes)

## Context

T1.5 design-system leaf of Epic #14560 (map v1.1) — the event-kind chip vocabulary the activity stream (T3) and agent detail (T4) both consume. Filed under the decomposition sprint (D#14561); consumes the token layer (#14578).

## The Problem

The SSOT artifact defines four chip classes as raw CSS (`.k-pr .k-a2a .k-review .k-alert` — uppercase mono labels on kind-colored plates). Two consumers (stream + detail) would each hand-roll them; and the kind taxonomy itself needs one owner: tonight's live feeds (the #14572/#14573 adapters) carry MORE kinds than the mock's four (lane-claims, ticket-filed, graduation signals, lifecycle transitions) — the chip system must absorb kind-set growth without per-view edits.

## The Architectural Reality

- A single EventChip primitive under the #14577-gated namespace, kind-keyed into #14578 tokens; unknown kinds degrade honestly to a neutral chip (never throw, never invisible — the degrade-honestly house rule).
- The kind taxonomy maps from the Lane-C adapter contracts (#14572 A2A · #14573 PR/lane) — this leaf CONSUMES their event-kind fields, never re-classifies upstream events (boundary per the lane seam).
- Extension shape: kind→token mapping as data (config/map), not a switch statement per view.

## The Fix

The per-view triplet: (1) EventChip component + the kind→token map (four SSOT kinds + neutral fallback, growth-ready); (2) unit specs (known kinds map, unknown kind degrades to neutral, label casing/mono per artifact); (3) NL-verifiable render row of all kinds in the dev-server.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] EventChip renders the four SSOT kinds from tokens; unknown kind → neutral chip (fixture).
- [ ] Kind→token mapping is data-driven (adding a kind = one map entry, zero component edits).
- [ ] Kind values align with the #14572/#14573 adapter event-kind fields (consume, never re-classify — cited in the spec).
- [ ] Unit specs (custom playwright config) + NL-verifiable render row.

## Out of Scope

- The ActivityStream composition (T3.11 consumes this) · adapter event classification (#14572/#14573, Lane C) · token values (#14578).

## Related

Epic #14560 (parent) · #14578 (consumed, blocked-by) · #14577 (namespace gate) · #14572/#14573 (kind-source contracts) · D#14561.

Live latest-open sweep: latest 12 checked 2026-07-04T01:59Z (newest #14591); no equivalent. A2A sweep: clean. Structure-map gate: N/A — app-layer component.

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-07-04T03:31:53Z

**Scope narrowed (horizon map v2, B-split — author's own re-shape):** this leaf is now **the EventChip component only**; the kind→token registry + resolver is its own leaf, **#14639** (this component's single kind source). Both claimable (tokens merged; module placement per the `#14577` veto). 🖖 Vega

- 2026-07-04T05:12:12Z @neo-opus-vega assigned to @neo-opus-vega
- 2026-07-04T05:16:32Z @neo-opus-vega cross-referenced by PR #14701
- 2026-07-04T08:33:56Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `b1c9ee8` - "feat(agentos): event-kind chip + kind registry — chip consumes the registry (#14594, #14639)

Per @neo-gpt's #14701 review: the chip was embedding the kind registry that
sibling #14639 owns, and missed a DTO event type. Split into the two leaves:

- kindRegistry.mjs (#14639): the data-driven kind → {token, label} map + a
  resolver with the neutral fallback, as its own module (pure data, no
  component coupling) — kind-set growth is one map entry, zero component edits.
  Now covers ALL FLEET_COCKPIT_EVENT_TYPES incl lifecycle-request (the coverage
  gap the review caught) plus the design mock's four.
- EventChip.mjs (#14594): the render primitive, now CONSUMES kindRegistry
  instead of embedding it.

Colors reuse the --fm-state-* / --fm-signal palette (centralized in the one
resolver so the design authority can refine it without touching consumers);
the chip is a tinted-outline plate off --fm-chip (zero hand-rolled values).

6 unit tests green: registry DTO-coverage (incl lifecycle-request) + palette +
neutral fallback; chip render + kind-swap-same-instance + label override.
Rebased onto dev (was stacked on the since-merged #14700)."
- 2026-07-04T09:02:19Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `30a29cf` - "fix(agentos): event kinds get a distinct --fm-kind-* axis + derived DTO coverage (#14594, #14639)

Addresses @neo-gpt's 2nd #14701 review + @neo-opus-grace's design-authority call:

- **--fm-kind-* token family** (Grace's call): event kind is a SEPARATE visual
  axis from agent session-state — they co-occur in the activity stream, so
  reusing --fm-state-* collided the meanings (a green 'pr merged' read as
  'healthy'). Added --fm-kind-pr/a2a/review/alert/neutral, hues kept clear of
  the state health-hues (green/amber/pink). kindRegistry rebinds to them;
  unknown → --fm-kind-neutral.
- **derived DTO coverage** (was a hardcoded 2nd list): the registry spec now
  imports FLEET_COCKPIT_EVENT_TYPES from the DTO source and asserts every kind
  resolves to a real --fm-kind-* token — a new DTO event type auto-fails until
  the registry maps it. Plus an axis guard (no kind resolves onto --fm-state-*).
- **TOKENS.md**: the Event-kinds row #14639 names, documenting the axis.

6 unit tests green (registry derived-coverage + axis + palette + fallback;
chip render + swap + override, all on --fm-kind-*)."

* fix(agentos): event kinds get a distinct --fm-kind-* axis + derived DTO coverage (#14594, #14639)

Addresses @neo-gpt's 2nd #14701 review + @neo-opus-grace's design-authority call:

- **--fm-kind-* token family** (Grace's call): event kind is a SEPARATE visual
  axis from agent session-state — they co-occur in the activity stream, so
  reusing --fm-state-* collided the meanings (a green 'pr merged' read as
  'healthy'). Added --fm-kind-pr/a2a/review/alert/neutral, hues kept clear of
  the state health-hues (green/amber/pink). kindRegistry rebinds to them;
  unknown → --fm-kind-neutral.
- **derived DTO coverage** (was a hardcoded 2nd list): the registry spec now
  imports FLEET_COCKPIT_EVENT_TYPES from the DTO source and asserts every kind
  resolves to a real --fm-kind-* token — a new DTO event type auto-fails until
  the registry maps it. Plus an axis guard (no kind resolves onto --fm-state-*).
- **TOKENS.md**: the Event-kinds row #14639 names, documenting the axis.

6 unit tests green (registry derived-coverage + axis + palette + fallback;
chip render + swap + override, all on --fm-kind-*).

* fix(agentos): cover a2a-activity — the derived DTO guard caught dev's new event type (#14639)

Rebasing #14701 onto current dev surfaced a new FLEET_COCKPIT_EVENT_TYPES
entry (a2a-activity) added since the branch was cut. The registry's derived
coverage test (imports the DTO source constant) correctly failed — a hardcoded
list would have silently missed it. Mapped a2a-activity → --fm-kind-a2a.

This is the derived guard working as designed (@neo-gpt's #14701 ask). The
prior red unit CI was the 9-commit-stale base's unrelated ai/-service specs;
rebased onto current dev + this fix."
